templates.name: Antiperspirant Lotions Shelf Life
templates.content: Most of our products have a shelf life of two or more years. Our Underarm Antiperspirant Lotions have a shelf life of one year and our Hand and Foot Antiperspirant Lotions have a shelf life of two years.

templates.name: Do you have a product for the butt area?
templates.content: Although our groin powder is designed for the groin area, lots of people see success using it for butt sweat as well! You can check it out here:  http://me.mycarpe.com/sl/19lgpKsF

templates.name: Can my child use Carpe?
templates.content: Our products are safe to use but we recommend them for children ages 8 and up. Most children will start using deodorant/antiperspirant around the age they begin puberty which can vary tremendously. We also suggest speaking with their doctor/dermatologist first to see if the excessive sweating isn't due to any underlying cause.

templates.name: Are there different types of hyperhidrosis?
templates.content: There are two forms of hyperhidrosis, known as secondary hyperhidrosis and primary hyperhidrosis. Primary hyperhidrosis is the most common form of hyperhidrosis, affecting approximately 15 to 20 million people in the United States alone. Secondary hyperhidrosis, although less common, is often indicative of another medical problem and is an issue for older individuals.

templates.name: How quickly will I see results?
templates.content: The truth is it depends. Some folks see results after their very first application. For others, thrice daily use for up to a month is necessary. Everybody's skin is different, and any genuine antiperspirant requires a certain amount of time and discipline to deliver results.

templates.name: How long does a tube last?
templates.content: A tube of Carpe lasts two months on average, with twice-daily usage.

templates.name: Will Carpe work for hyperhidrosis?
templates.content: While no cure or treatment yet exists for the root cause of hyperhidrosis (which researchers believe originates in the brain), Carpe was specifically made to help manage the sweating caused by hyperhidrosis of the hands and feet, also known as palmar and plantar, or palmoplantar hyperhidrosis. Hyperhidrosis causes moist hands and feet through sweating, and Carpe can be a huge help in reducing this sweating!

templates.name: It feels sticky, am I doing something wrong?
templates.content: Carpe may feel sticky if you apply too much of it. Be sure to only use a pea-sized amount, and the stickiness will go away within a minute of application.

templates.name: How do I use the Lotion for my feet?
templates.content: For best results apply to dry feet every night right before bed, and reapply in the morning. You can reapply throughout the day for even more sweat reduction. You will see maximum effects after 4 weeks, then the frequency of application can be reduced.

templates.name: Is hyperhidrosis dangerous or harmful to my body like other conditions?
templates.content: Although hyperhidrosis is not inherently dangerous or harmful to your physical health, the condition's impact can be significant. From elementary school children who are afraid to raise their hands at school to teenagers and young adults who cannot write an essay or draw a picture without sweat ruining the paper, hyperhidrosis poses a serious problem. Many individuals with hyperhidrosis struggle to shake hands, speak in public or even maintain confidence.

templates.name: How do I use the Lotion for my hands?
templates.content: Wash and dry your hands and apply a pea-sized amount of Carpe to your palms. (too much can feel sticky!). Then rub your palms together for 15 seconds.

templates.name: How do I use the Lotion for my underarm sweat?
templates.content: Apply to dry underarms every night before bed and again in the morning. If you need additional sweat blocking, reapply throughout the day. Testers saw a median of 60% reduction in sweat after just 3 applications.

templates.name: How many wipes in the Underarm Antiperspirant Wipes?
templates.content: There are 15 individual wipes in each box of our Underarm Antiperspirant Wipes.

templates.name: Are your products safe to use while breastfeeding/pregnant?
templates.content: Carpe is not tested for use while pregnant or breastfeeding. We advise consulting with your doctor before using our products. We'll be happy to send your doctor a sample so that they are more familiar with our products as well!

templates.content: We don't offer payment plan options at this time.

templates.name: How do I change the frequency of my subscription?
templates.content:
To edit the frequency of your subscription:

- Log in to your customer portal: https://mycarpe.com/tools/recurring/get-subscription-access
- Click on the "Manage subscriptions" tab.
- Under the subscription you wish to edit, select "Manage this subscription."
- You can then adjust the quantity of products and the frequency. Once done, select "Save changes" to confirm the adjustment.

templates.name: What do I do if the product doesn't work for me?
templates.content: We believe in our product and want to create sweat solutions that work for everyone, so your purchase of Carpe comes with a lifetime 100% money back guarantee, which means that if you don't like your Carpe item(s) for any reason, we will fully refund your returned purchase. Shipping costs not included. You can check out our detailed return policy here: http://me.mycarpe.com/sl/iExfUICU

templates.name: Does your product contain aluminum?
templates.content:
Carpe contains aluminum salt. Antiperspirants, like Carpe, are also mandated by the FDA and must contain an aluminum salt as an active ingredient to be considered an "antiperspirant". The aluminum salt is what works to safely reduce sweat in the sweat glands.

Please feel free to also check out this link to learn more about the aluminum used in all antiperspirants. http://me.mycarpe.com/aHurTOZFzvRc
